labels.no_insights: No notable differences between the two runs.

templates.lower_vram: {run} uses less VRAM (avg {low} MB vs {high} MB).
templates.failed_run: {run} did not complete successfully; comparison is limited.
templates.recommend_tie: Runs are close; either config works on this machine.

status.tight: fits GPU only with a shorter context

suggestions.good_fit: This configuration should fit your GPU.
suggestions.reduce_context: Reduce context length to about {target} tokens.
suggestions.lower_quant: Use a lower-bit quantization (for example q4 or q3).
suggestions.cpu_offload: Use CPU/RAM offload; the weights fit in system RAM.
suggestions.no_cpu_offload: System RAM is also tight; a smaller model is safer.
suggestions.run_hardware: GPU VRAM was not detected; run `doctor hardware` or pass --vram.

notes.approximate: Estimates are approximate; KV cache and overhead are heuristic.
notes.moe: Mixture-of-experts model detected; total parameters may differ. Pass --params for accuracy.

backend_failed.title: Backend benchmark failed
backend_failed.detail: The model run did not complete. Metrics were still collected until the failure.

gpu_missing.title: NVIDIA GPU metrics could not be read
gpu_missing.detail: nvidia-smi may be missing, or this system may not have an NVIDIA GPU.

gpu_idle.title: GPU appears idle
gpu_idle.detail: GPU average and peak utilization are low. The model may be running on CPU or not reaching the GPU.

ram_pressure.title: RAM pressure detected
ram_pressure.detail: RAM usage reached a critical level. Swap/pagefile may have been involved.

ram_pressure_soft.title: RAM usage is high
ram_pressure_soft.detail: Average RAM usage is high. Larger models or longer contexts are risky.

vram_bottleneck.title: VRAM bottleneck likely ({ratio}%)
vram_bottleneck.detail: Peak VRAM usage reached {ratio}% of total capacity.

vram_high.title: VRAM usage is high ({ratio}%)
vram_high.detail: VRAM headroom is limited. Larger context or model size may hit the limit.

cpu_inference_likely.title: CPU inference likely
cpu_inference_likely.detail: CPU usage is meaningful while GPU usage is low or unavailable. Inference may have fallen back to CPU.

no_clear_bottleneck.title: No clear critical bottleneck
no_clear_bottleneck.detail: This run did not show an obvious CPU/RAM/GPU/VRAM issue from the basic metrics.

recommendations.backend_failed: Check that the backend service is running, the base URL/model name are correct, and the model can be loaded.
recommendations.gpu_idle: Check whether the backend loaded the model onto the GPU, driver/CUDA health, and model quantization choice.
recommendations.cpu_inference_likely: If GPU inference is expected, inspect backend logs and GPU offload behavior; if CPU inference is expected, try a smaller or more quantized model.
recommendations.ram_pressure: Try a smaller model, lower context, or more aggressive quantization; close background applications.
recommendations.ram_pressure_soft: RAM may climb on longer prompts; increase context and model size gradually.
recommendations.vram_bottleneck: Reduce context length, choose a smaller or lower-bit quantized model, and close other GPU workloads.
recommendations.vram_high: Reduce context or model size before VRAM gets too close to the limit.
recommendations.no_clear_bottleneck: Run again with longer realistic prompts; use `doctor compare` to compare two runs side by side.

advisor.disabled_summary: Advisor output was skipped for this run.

no_issue.title: No high-priority issue was selected
no_issue.summary: The diagnosis did not produce one of the tracked advisor issues, so only the diagnosis report was shown.
recommendations: Use a longer, more realistic benchmark prompt to produce stronger diagnosis signals.
Compare multiple runs to see whether the pattern is stable.
Review the raw diagnosis findings before changing runtime settings.

templates.ram_max: RAM peaked at {max}% with an average of {avg}%.
templates.vram_ratio_high: Peak VRAM usage reached {ratio}% of available VRAM.
templates.vram_ratio_low: Peak VRAM usage stayed low at {ratio}% of available VRAM.

gpu_idle.title: The GPU may not be used effectively.
gpu_idle.summary: This run looks like the model is not fully reaching the GPU path during inference.
evidence: GPU usage stayed low during the benchmark.
VRAM usage stayed low during the benchmark.
recommendations: Check the GPU acceleration setting in Ollama or LM Studio.
Check NVIDIA driver and CUDA health.
Run the same benchmark again while the GPU path is confirmed active.

ram_pressure.title: RAM pressure.
ram_pressure.summary: System memory usage is high enough to threaten stable inference, especially on longer prompts or larger models.
evidence: RAM usage reached a critical level.
The current run may be close to swap or pagefile pressure.
recommendations: Try a smaller model or a shorter context window.
Close other memory-heavy applications before rerunning the benchmark.
Repeat the benchmark with the same prompt after lowering memory pressure.

ram_pressure_soft.title: Rising RAM pressure.
ram_pressure_soft.summary: Memory is not fully critical yet, but the run is already close enough to matter for larger workloads.
evidence: Average RAM usage is already high.
Longer prompts or larger models may push the system into memory pressure.
recommendations: Watch memory usage on longer prompts before scaling model size.
Reduce context length if later runs become unstable.
Keep a baseline run so later regressions are easy to spot.

vram_bottleneck.title: A VRAM bottleneck.
vram_bottleneck.summary: GPU memory usage is close to the hardware limit, which can block larger contexts or heavier models.
evidence: VRAM usage reached a critical share of available GPU memory.
The current configuration leaves little headroom for larger workloads.
recommendations: Reduce context length or batch size.
Use a smaller or more aggressively quantized model.
Repeat the benchmark after freeing GPU memory from other workloads.

vram_high.title: High VRAM usage.
vram_high.summary: The GPU path is active, but VRAM headroom is getting tight and larger runs may hit the limit.
evidence: VRAM usage is already high for this benchmark.
There is limited headroom for larger prompts or models.
recommendations: Watch VRAM usage before increasing context length.
Prefer a smaller or more efficient model if this run is only a baseline.
Repeat the same benchmark after reducing other GPU activity.

cpu_inference_likely.title: Likely CPU-side inference.
cpu_inference_likely.summary: The run suggests the workload is leaning on CPU execution when a GPU path was probably expected.
evidence: CPU usage is meaningful during the run.
GPU usage is low or unavailable compared with the CPU signal.
recommendations: Check whether the model is actually configured to use GPU acceleration.
Review backend logs for GPU offload or fallback messages.
Repeat the same benchmark after confirming the runtime is using the GPU.
